To identify stocks (on a 20-day high) popping up, you've chosen to screen for change in price (e.g., 1% or 2 % for change and change from open). Perhaps I'm overthinking it, but a new 20-day high candlestick could be <1% change from yesterday because it opened lower, yet still be a significant >2% change from open. And would screening based on volume, e.g., relative chosen >1.5 also work?

Winning screener set up .    .... first...52 week high /low...new highs....50day..new high...20 day..new high...200 day mA..must be above it..ps ratio less than 1...market cap md caps or lower..country USA...sales q/q 30%>...these presets has led me to top stocks... everything else is on any..

I have a question regarding FinViz. I only subscribed to it because of watching your tutorial videos and searching for stocks. My new annoying concern is bank charges. When i google finfiz it says its a company located in NewYork. Im okay with paying my 3 month fee of $39.50, but why are they also charging me an additional $1.19 some 4 days later for a  INTERNATIONAL TRANSACTION FEE 02/17 FINVIZ LTD. BRATISLAVA ? It says its a Bank Charge fee from Bratislava. To me this sounds very sketchy when the business is here registered in the US and paying US taxes why a i paying a bank fee located in Bratislava?
So boring and repetitive. Stick to the point and talk less.
Thanks Pete, I do use Finviz. But you have provided some great value that I will incorporate into screening analysis.
